channel.title: Inner-Products on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Inner-Products on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: Inner Product Spaces and Hilbert Spaces
item.description:
Geometry in infinite dimensions#

Banach spaces have distance and linear structure, but no notion of angle. You can’t talk about orthogonality in $\ell^1$ or $C[0,1]$ (with sup norm) in any natural way. To get geometry — perpendicularity, projections, least-squares approximation — you need an inner product.

The inner product is what lets you decompose a vector into components along orthogonal directions. In $\mathbb{R}^n$ this is routine linear algebra. In infinite dimensions it becomes Fourier analysis, and the abstract framework unifies everything from trigonometric series to wavelet decompositions to quantum state expansions.
